title: Pseudopodospermum libanoticum
taxonomy: Pseudopodospermum libanoticum is a species in the family Celastraceae. It is classified under the kingdom Plantae and is a type of flowering plant. The plant is further classified into the order Celastrales, and then into the class Magnoliopsida. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and morphological features. Pseudopodospermum libanoticum's classification is also reflected in its botanical name, which follows the binomial nomenclature system.

intro: Pseudopodospermum libanoticum, also known as the Lebanese Storax tree, is a small, evergreen tree or shrub native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions, including wounds, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Pseudopodospermum libanoticum include volatile oils, particularly cinnamic aldehyde, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pseudopodospermum libanoticum is commonly used to make extracts, tinctures, and essential oils, which are often used to treat skin conditions, respiratory issues, and as a natural insect repellent.
